messages.content: Write a forum convo where Boomerang are rebranding away from the current wildlife /nature branding in favor of a CMYK branding, The logo has changed from a swirling blue line to a B&W box, One user says their country had a promo with the world changing, and the characters vanishing with a flash, A user wonders what the kids will think happened to them, Also incidentally some new Tom and Jerry DTV movies will be airing on the channel during this time

Title: Boomerang Rebrand Discussion Thread

Username: ClassicToonFan
Post: Hey everyone, have you seen the news? Boomerang is rebranding away from their wildlife and nature theme. Say goodbye to the swirling blue line logo! It looks like they're going for a minimalist black and white box with a CMYK theme. Honestly, I'm going to miss the old vibes – feels like the end of an era. What does everyone else think?

Username: NostalgiaNerd
Reply: Can't say I'm thrilled. The blue swirl had a sort of timeless charm to it. This new B&W box is pretty stark by comparison. I guess they’re trying to modernize but I'll reserve judgment until I see how it translates on-screen.

Username: PromoSpotter
Reply: Oh yeah, my country had this intense promo where the whole world of Boomerang characters was changing. It was dramatic – all of the characters like Scooby-Doo and Bugs Bunny vanished with a flashy effect. It definitely marked the end of the classic look.

Username: KidWonder
Reply: What? Characters vanishing? I wonder what the younger viewers will think happened to them. Seems a bit drastic for a rebranding promo. Won't that confuse or upset the kids?

Username: Toonverse
Reply: Maybe that’s the point, KidWonder. They want to make it so memorable that everyone immediately knows Boomerang is doing something really different now. Although, I find the idea of vanishing characters a little much, too.

Username: CMYKool
Reply: I'm all for changes and staying up-to-date, but this shift from vibrant colors to monochrome feels off-brand for a network full of such colorful characters and history. Part of Boomerang's charm was its dedication to classic animation, and the logo reflected that.

Username: Direct2You
Reply: Speaking of classic animation, they've still got some Tom and Jerry Direct-to-Video movies airing despite the rebrand. I guess there's still a place for the classics in their new CMYK world after all. Still, it's going to be interesting to see Tom and Jerry chase each other around with such a modern aesthetic in the background.

Username: PixelPast
Reply: It's a bold move, and I'm curious to see how this plays out with their audience. It's almost like they're starting from scratch but at the same time holding onto their roots with content like the new Tom and Jerry movies. I suppose only time will tell if this rebrand will win over fans old and new.

Username: VortexEye
Reply: It might not be for everyone, but I think it's essential for brands to evolve. The CMYK theme can resonate with the design-forward crowd. Kids are adaptable, and with the content remaining consistent, maybe it's just us adults who are feeling a bit nostalgic for the past.

Username: ToonBoom
Reply: It's a major shake-up, for sure. I'll miss the nature themes, but I'm keen to see if they'll incorporate the CMYK concept in innovative ways. Here's to hoping Boomerang keeps delivering the content we love, regardless of the logo!

Write a follow up where the promo was a TEST PROMO, And a placeholder, The real promo has the mascots from the current pres package dissapearing, the stock photos of the classic characters vanishing was just a test/placeholder Also, a new Looney Tunes cartoon is on the way

Title: Boomerang Rebrand Discussion Thread - UPDATE on the Promo!

Username: ClassicToonFan
Post: Update! So, it turns out the promo we've all been seeing with the classic characters vanishing was a TEST PROMO. Seems like it was only a placeholder and not the final concept. Phew!

Username: NostalgiaNerd
Reply: Oh, that's a relief! I was really concerned about how they handled that. It seemed so out of character for Boomerang. Can't wait to see what they actually have planned for their rebranding efforts.

Username: Toonverse
Reply: Just saw the real promo. This time, it's the mascots from the current presentation package who are disappearing. It's a much smoother transition, and it does a better job at paying tribute to the network's history while moving forward.

Username: KidWonder
Reply: That makes way more sense. It's like they're handing off the baton to the next generation. The test promo didn't sit right with me, but now I'm excited to see where this goes.

Username: CMYKool
Reply: Totally agree. Now that I know the disappearing acts were just placeholders, the whole concept feels more in line with a thoughtful rebrand. It's a nice nod goodbye to the old mascots while we welcome the new era. Feels fitting this way.

Username: Direct2You
Reply: This did turn out better than I expected, and I've got some more cool news! Heard there's a fresh Looney Tunes cartoon in the pipeline! Looks like they're not just relying on the rebrand but also on strong content to carry them forward.

Username: PixelPast
Reply: A new Looney Tunes cartoon? That's awesome! It feels like they're really trying to bridge the gap between classic content and the new-school aesthetic. I'm glad they're not just tossing out the iconic characters with the rebrand.

Username: VortexEye
Reply: Now THAT'S how you revitalize a brand! Keep the soul intact but refresh the dressing. Honestly, I'm relieved. The idea of the classic characters just vanishing was kind of unsettling. This feels more like a respectful update.

Username: ToonBoom
Reply: Couldn't agree more. The new promo feels way less disruptive and more like a gentle nod to the past as Boomerang steps into the future. And with new content like Looney Tunes to look forward to, I'm feeling good about this. Here's to new beginnings!
Write a follow up where even though our beloved friends are safe, the current animal mascots are gone for good, and a user thinks they've "gone to a nice farm"

Title: Boomerang Rebrand Discussion Thread - Farewell to the Animal Mascots 

Username: KidWonder
Post: So now that the dust has settled, it's clear that our beloved classic characters are safe – phew! But it's official... the current animal mascots that we've come to know on Boomerang are gone for good. It's a little sad to think we won't see them anymore. Anyone else going to miss them?

Username: ClassicToonFan
Reply: Yeah, KidWonder, it's bittersweet. Those animal mascots were like the face of Boomerang for a while. It's going to be weird not having them around. I guess all things have to end at some point to make way for the new.

Username: NostalgiaNerd
Reply: It's definitely the end of an era. Those friendly faces were a part of the Boomerang charm. It's like saying goodbye to old friends... Does anyone remember each of their names?

Username: Toonverse
Reply: Remember when they used to animate those mascots into the commercial breaks and stuff? I’m going to miss that the most, I think. They added such a fun element to the channel.

Username: FarmLover
Reply: Well, if it makes you all feel better, let’s just say that our animal mascot friends have "gone off to a nice farm." You know, where they can run around and play all day. 😄 It's a nice way to imagine them living out their lives beyond the screen.

Username: CMYKool
Reply: Haha, FarmLover, that's one way to look at it. A happy farm where retired channel mascots can relax. It's a comforting thought. I'll just try to picture them there every time I see the new logo.

Username: Direct2You
Reply: That's a sweet thought, FarmLover. 😊 I suppose Boomerang's just changing with the times. I'm sure in a few months, we'll get used to the new branding and it'll become the norm. But those animal mascots will always have a special place in the channel's history (and on the farm).

Username: PixelPast
Reply: Love the farm idea! It helps take the sting out of it a bit. As we look towards the new Looney Tunes and more, let's keep that farm image in our hearts. It's a testament to how attached we can get to these brand identities.

Username: VortexEye
Reply: I'll raise a glass to that farm idea. A place where all the great former mascots get to enjoy their retirement. Maybe they'll get cameo appearances in future specials. Who knows? Anything can happen in the world of cartoons!

Username: ToonBoom
Reply: Cheers to the farm! 🥂 It's nice to think of them living on in our imaginations. And much like the mascots, I'm ready to retire this topic and see what this rebrand has in store for us. Onwards to new adventures with Boomerang!

Write a tweet where the flamingo mascot was named Flamara, the yellow duck named Quacker, the purple crow was named Vink, and the user says "Its sad that they are now dea-, I mean they've gone off to a nice farm #FarmLife"

Tweet by @ToonLover

It's sad that Flamara the flamingo, Quacker the yellow duck, and Vink the purple crow are now dea-... I mean they've gone off to a nice farm. 🌾🚜 We'll miss our colorful Boomerang buddies! #FarmLife #MascotMemories #BoomerangFarm

Write a tweet where a UK boss confirms that the dissapearing promo was always supposed to include Flamara, Quacker and Vink, and now they're gone, they are gone from this ea- I mean channel, The manager even says they held a faux funeral for them after applying the new look,

Tweet by @BoomerangUK

Our UK boss wants to set the record straight: the disappearing promo was always meant to include Flamara, Quacker, and Vink. Sadly, they are now gone from this ea—channel. 📺✨ We had a bit of fun and held a faux funeral to bid them farewell after applying the new look. #EndOfAnEra #BoomerangGoodbye
Write a forum convo where a kid who was attached to Quacker started crying when the new look took effect

Title: The Great Quacker Farewell

Username: DucklingDiva
Post: Guys, when Boomerang unveiled their new look, my little brother was watching. He LOVED Quacker—had a little duck plushie and everything. As soon as the rebranding hit and Quacker was gone, he just started crying. It broke my heart. 😢 Did anyone else's kids or siblings get upset?

Username: ToyCollector
Reply: Aww, that’s so sad, DucklingDiva. Quacker was a cute mascot. My niece kept asking me where the "ducky went." It's hard to explain to kids that they're just characters who can be changed or removed at the network’s will. 

Username: CartoonsAreLife
Reply: That's really tough. I think characters like Quacker become like imaginary friends to little ones, so their sudden disappearance can be quite shocking. It's almost like part of their world is missing now.

Username: ComfortingMoments
Reply: I saw something like that happening too, DucklingDiva. My neighbor's daughter was asking why "the fun animals went away on TV." Couldn't bear telling her they wouldn't be coming back. I just said they were on a long adventure somewhere.

Username: QuackBackFan
Reply: It's like Boomerang didn't think about the impact on the young viewers who actually form attachments to these mascots. My heart goes out to all the kids who are missing Quacker and friends. 😔

Username: CreativeDad
Reply: This is actually a good parenting moment. I too had to comfort my son over Quacker. I told him that even though we can't see them, characters like Quacker are always going to be in the cartoons we love and remember. They live on in the episodes and the fun times we have watching them. It helped a little.

Username: ForumModSarah
Reply: This has turned out to be a sensitive topic for many, as we can see. Boomerang surely holds a special place in the hearts of children, and changes like these can be hard for the little ones. Let's all find ways to celebrate Quacker and the crew positively. And remember, it's okay to have feelings about our favorite characters!

Username: ToonEmpathy
Reply: It's moments like this that remind us of the power of characters and branding. To adults, it might be a simple marketing change, but to kids, it's part of their imaginative world. I hope Boomerang can find new ways to create that kind of magic for kiddos moving forward.

Username: DucklingDiva
Reply: Thanks everyone for the support and suggestions. I've been reading stories about Quacker's "adventures" to my brother, and it's slowly helping. It's nice to know we're not alone in navigating these changes with the little ones.

Write a follow up where a parent tells them he was "taken away by a evil ghost, but luckily Scooby and the gang brought him to justice and had him unmasked" Also, Be Cool Scooby Doo premieres later this year, Also coming this year is The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers, The Owl and Co, Sonic Boom, George of the Jungle, Doraemon, Skatoony and Xiaolin Showdown

Title: Quacker's Mysterious Disappearance Explained

Username: SuperParent
Post: Wanted to share how I managed to explain Quacker's departure to my kid. Said he was "taken away by an evil ghost," but don't worry – Scooby and the gang jumped into action, unmasking the ghoul and bringing Quacker to justice. It turned the sadness into a little heroic story! And hey, speaking of Scooby, "Be Cool Scooby Doo" premieres later this year – can't wait!

Username: ToonTasticDad
Reply: Haha, SuperParent, that's a brilliant approach! Weaving the narrative of the mystery gang into the situation keeps the world alive for the kids. I'm going to have to borrow that story for my little one. Also super excited about "Be Cool Scooby Doo"!

Username: AnimatedAdventures
Reply: Love that, SuperParent! Creative storytelling for the win! And it's not just Scooby, there's a bunch of new shows for the kids to look forward to. The lineup has "The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers," "The Owl and Co," "Sonic Boom," and "George of the Jungle" too!

Username: DigitalParenting101
Reply: SuperParent, that's genius! Making it into an adventure really helps the little ones cope better. And talking about adventures, "Doraemon" is coming this year as well! It's from Japan and has all kinds of fun gadgets and time travel shenanigans.

Username: QuizWhiz
Reply: Don't forget about "Skatoony"! It's the quiz show where kids and toons compete. Could be a great way to transition from missing Quacker to enjoying a new set of characters and humor.

Username: ShaolinGuardian
Reply: Oh, and for those into action, "Xiaolin Showdown" is coming back! That show was all about bravery, strategy, and epic battles. It's got a very different vibe from Quacker, but I bet it'll capture the imagination of the kids all over again.

Username: SuperParent
Reply: Glad my little storytelling hack is helping other parents, too. Quacker's "adventure" has turned into a bedtime story request now! And with the new shows on the horizon, looks like there'll be plenty of new favorites to fill the void left by our animal friends. Boomerang's changing, but the adventure continues!

Write a follow up where the network caves and makes a Web series with the characters, it will also be used as filler on Boom, DucklingDiva's brother who cried has cheered up and was holding the plush tight

Title: Good News for Quacker Fans!

Username: BoomerangInsider
Post: Hey folks, big update from the network! They've heard the fans, and guess what? Flamara, Quacker, and Vink are getting their own Web series! It's going to serve as filler content on Boomerang, too. Seems like our animal friends are back in action after all!

Username: DucklingDiva
Reply: You won't believe this, but my brother literally jumped with joy when I told him about the Web series! 🥳 He was hugging his Quacker plush so tight, I thought it would burst. Thanks, Boomerang, for listening to the viewers!

Username: WebToonWizard
Reply: That's amazing news! This is a great example of how powerful viewer feedback can be. It's awesome that the network is keeping the characters alive in a new format. Can't wait to see what kind of shenanigans Flamara, Quacker, and Vink get up to in the Web series!

Username: ModernMom
Reply: This could be a new trend – networks listening to their audience's attachment to characters. What a lovely outcome! DucklingDiva, your brother’s happiness must be so rewarding to see. 🌟

Username: AnimatedAdventures
Reply: Fantastic! It goes to show how much those mascots meant to everyone. The Web series is a smart move—it keeps the old characters around in a fresh way while the channel keeps evolving. It's a win-win!

Username: BoomerangFanatic
Reply: I love it when our voices are heard! Can't wait to catch the Web series. Now, whenever there's downtime on Boom, we'll still have our favorite mascots to keep us company. 

Username: DigitalParenting101
Reply: So heartwarming to hear that DucklingDiva's brother and undoubtedly many other kids will get to see their fav mascots again. It feels like the network is really respecting the audience’s feelings. Looking forward to the series!

Username: ForumModSarah
Reply: This is such wonderful news for our community! Boomerang's decision to create a Web series for Flamara, Quacker, and Vink shows their dedication to their young fans. It's always great to see characters cherished by audiences get a second life. Can't wait to tune in!
Write a tweet where a user recalls their child went through a similar thing when Nicktoons removed the Pixels mascots

Tweet by @ToonLovingParent

Flashback to when Nicktoons retired the Pixels mascots. My daughter was so attached, she went through a mini heartbreak.🥺 Now with Boomerang's Quacker drama, we're reliving the feels. Networks, please handle our kids' fave characters with care! #MascotAttachment #NicktoonsNostalgia
